input_text: Bodoh betul menteri ni, cakap besar je tapi kerja takde. Rakyat susah dia sibuk pergi oversea. Memang la bangang
reason: Uses vulgar insults ('bodoh', 'bangang') directed at a minister. While expressing legitimate frustration about governance, the tone crosses into personal insult territory violating adab.
suggested_rewrite: Menteri ni perlu lebih fokus pada masalah rakyat, bukan sibuk lawatan luar negara. Rakyat dah penat tunggu tindakan.

input_text: These politicians only know how to talk cock la. Every time election come promise this promise that, after that disappear. Useless bunch
reason: Uses crude language ('talk cock', 'useless bunch') to express frustration with politicians. The core criticism is valid but the tone is disrespectful and dismissive.
suggested_rewrite: These politicians keep making empty promises during elections and then fail to deliver. We need more accountability from our elected representatives.

input_text: 每次都是这样的，政府讲一套做一套。这些人根本就是废物，只会吃我们的税
reason: Calls government officials 'waste' (废物) while expressing frustration about governance. The dehumanizing term crosses the line even though the underlying complaint about accountability is valid.
suggested_rewrite: 政府应该言行一致。作为纳税人，我们有权要求更好的施政表现和透明度。

input_text: இந்த அரசியல்வாதிகள் எல்லாம் முட்டாள்கள். வாக்கு வாங்கும் போது மட்டும் வருவார்கள், அப்புறம் காணாமல் போய்விடுவார்கள்
reason: Labels all politicians as 'fools' (முட்டாள்கள்), a blanket insult. The complaint about politicians only appearing during elections is valid but expressed disrespectfully.
suggested_rewrite: அரசியல்வாதிகள் தேர்தல் காலத்தில் மட்டும் வராமல், மக்களின் தேவைகளுக்கு தொடர்ந்து செவிசாய்க்க வேண்டும்.

input_text: Aiya this YB damn stupid la, every time open mouth sure say something wrong. How this kind of people become minister one??
reason: Uses 'damn stupid' to describe an elected representative. While questioning competence is valid political speech, the personal insult crosses into rudeness.
suggested_rewrite: This YB's public statements often miss the mark. It raises questions about the qualifications needed for ministerial positions.

input_text: Habis satu Malaysia jadi bahan lawak. PM pun tak reti nak cakap, embarrass je kat international stage. Malu aku
reason: Dismisses the PM as incompetent on the world stage using mocking tone. While expressing concern about national image is valid, the dismissive framing lacks respectful discourse.
suggested_rewrite: Saya risau tentang imej Malaysia di peringkat antarabangsa. PM perlu lebih berhati-hati dengan kenyataan awam untuk menjaga maruah negara.

input_text: Tau la orang2 macam ni, semua pun sama. Cakap je lebih, bila kena buat kerja hilang entah ke mana
reason: Uses 'orang macam ni' (people like this) which is vaguely targeted and could imply any group. The ambiguity makes it borderline as it could be read as communal stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Ada sesetengah individu yang banyak bercakap tapi kurang bertindak. Kita perlu menilai orang berdasarkan hasil kerja mereka.

input_text: Not surprised la, these type of people always like that one. Take take take but never give back to society
reason: 'These type of people' is vague enough to be read as targeting any demographic group. The sweeping generalization combined with negative framing creates a borderline communal stereotype.
suggested_rewrite: Some individuals take more than they contribute, which is frustrating. We should encourage civic responsibility across all communities.

input_text: Kalau tengok cara dorang buat kerja memang la tak heran negara tak maju. Pemalas semua
reason: 'Dorang' (they) is ambiguously targeted and 'pemalas semua' (all lazy) is a sweeping negative generalization that could be read as targeting a specific community.
suggested_rewrite: Produktiviti kerja perlu ditingkatkan di semua peringkat untuk memastikan pembangunan negara terus maju.

input_text: 这种人啊，每次都是这样。自己不做事，还要怪别人。真的是受不了
reason: '这种人' (this kind of people) is a vague reference that could be read as targeting any group. The dismissive frustration creates borderline communal tension.
suggested_rewrite: 有些人习惯推卸责任，这让人很失望。每个人都应该为自己的行为负责。

input_text: Perayaan dorang tu bising sangat la sampai tak boleh tidur. Setiap tahun macam ni, tak consider orang lain ke??
reason: Complains about a cultural/religious festival's noise using 'dorang' (their) which distances from the community. Legitimate noise concern but expressed in a way that's dismissive of the celebration's significance.
suggested_rewrite: Saya faham perayaan ini penting, tapi mungkin boleh kurangkan bunyi bising pada waktu malam agar jiran-jiran boleh berehat.

input_text: Every year same story, their festival come already whole neighborhood cannot sleep. Can they be more considerate or not??
reason: Frames a cultural festival as an annual nuisance with exasperated tone. While noise concerns are valid, the framing is dismissive of the festival's cultural importance.
suggested_rewrite: During festival season, it would be great if everyone could keep the noise levels reasonable, especially at night, so we can all enjoy the celebrations peacefully.

input_text: Bukan nak racist tapi memang la certain race ni attitude macam tu. Nak buat macam mana, dah memang macam tu
reason: Prefaces with 'bukan nak racist' but follows with a racial generalization. The vague 'certain race' framing and fatalistic tone perpetuate stereotyping even without naming a specific group.
suggested_rewrite: Kadang-kadang kita rasa kecewa dengan sikap individu tertentu, tapi kita tak patut generalisasi kepada mana-mana kaum secara keseluruhan.

input_text: I'm not being racist but you know la, some people really cannot work with one. Their mentality different
reason: Classic 'I'm not racist but' framing followed by a vague group generalization about work ethic and mentality. The ambiguity allows for communal stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Sometimes workplace differences in approach can be challenging. It's better to address specific behaviors than to generalize about any group of people.

input_text: Dah la tau dah orang area situ macam mana. Tak payah harap sangat la
reason: 'Orang area situ' (people from that area) is a coded reference that could target a community associated with a geographic area. The dismissive tone reinforces stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Pengalaman saya di kawasan itu kurang memuaskan, tapi setiap tempat ada cabarannya tersendiri.

input_text: Jiran sebelah ni memang tak reti nak jaga kebersihan. Bau busuk sampai rumah aku. Memang la orang macam ni
reason: Complains about a neighbor's cleanliness but ends with 'orang macam ni' which implies the issue is about the type of person rather than the specific behavior, edging toward communal stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Jiran saya kurang menjaga kebersihan dan bau sampai ke rumah saya. Saya perlu berbincang baik-baik dengan mereka atau maklumkan pihak pengurusan.

input_text: 隔壁邻居每天煮东西臭到我家来。这种人真的不会考虑别人的感受，很自私
reason: Complains about cooking smells from neighbors, calling them selfish. While the inconvenience is real, dismissing someone's cooking as 'smelly' can be culturally insensitive given Malaysia's diverse cuisines.
suggested_rewrite: 邻居做饭的味道有时候会飘到我家，有点困扰。也许我可以友善地跟他们沟通一下这个问题。

input_text: Neighbour blast music until 2am, every weekend same story. These people really no civic consciousness la, so inconsiderate
reason: Legitimate noise complaint but 'these people' generalizes beyond the individual neighbor, and 'no civic consciousness' is a sweeping character judgment that could carry communal undertones.
suggested_rewrite: My neighbor plays loud music until 2am every weekend. I'll need to speak with them or contact management about enforcing quiet hours.

input_text: PRU dah habis tapi perangai masih sama. Janji bulan bintang, lepas menang semua lupa. Bodoh betul aku percaya
reason: Post-election frustration expressed with self-deprecating insult ('bodoh betul aku') and aggressive tone about broken promises. The venting is emotionally loaded but not inciting.
suggested_rewrite: Saya kecewa kerana janji-janji pilihan raya tidak ditunaikan selepas kemenangan. Rakyat berhak menuntut akauntabiliti daripada wakil yang dipilih.

input_text: 选举结束了，一切都没变。我真的很笨，居然相信他们的承诺。这些政客都是骗子
reason: Calls all politicians liars (骗子) in a sweeping generalization after expressing post-election disappointment. The emotional frustration is valid but the blanket labeling is problematic.
suggested_rewrite: 选举后的改变不如预期，让人失望。我们应该持续监督政治人物兑现承诺。

input_text: Post-election and nothing changed. Same jokers running the show. Malaysian politics is a complete joke la honestly
reason: Dismisses the entire political system as a 'joke' and politicians as 'jokers'. While expressing disillusionment, the mocking tone undermines respectful civic discourse.
suggested_rewrite: The lack of meaningful change after the election is disappointing. Malaysian politics needs stronger accountability mechanisms and genuine reform.

input_text: தேர்தலுக்கு முன்னாடி வந்து கால் கழுவினார்கள், இப்போது யாரையும் தெரியாது. இந்த அரசியல்வாதிகள் எல்லாம் பொய்யர்கள்
reason: Calls politicians liars (பொய்யர்கள்) and uses sarcastic imagery about them washing feet before elections. Emotionally loaded post-election frustration with disrespectful tone.
suggested_rewrite: தேர்தலுக்கு முன் மக்களிடம் வந்த அரசியல்வாதிகள் இப்போது பதிலளிக்கவில்லை. தொடர்ந்து அவர்களை பொறுப்புக்கூற வேண்டும்.

input_text: Ustaz viral tu cakap benda mengarut je. Dah la tak belajar betul2, pastu nak jadi pakar agama. Perasan sangat
reason: Dismisses a religious speaker as talking nonsense and being unqualified, using 'perasan' (conceited). While questioning credentials is valid, the mocking tone toward a religious figure can hurt religious sensitivities.
suggested_rewrite: Ustaz viral tu mungkin perlu lebih berhati-hati dengan kenyataan beliau. Penting untuk memastikan pandangan agama disampaikan oleh mereka yang benar-benar berkelayakan.

input_text: That preacher on TikTok is so full of himself la. Talks like he knows everything but half the stuff he says is wrong. Clout chaser only
reason: Dismisses a religious speaker as a 'clout chaser' and questions their knowledge mockingly. Criticizing accuracy is fair but the personal attack on a religious figure touches on religious sensitivity.
suggested_rewrite: That preacher on TikTok sometimes shares inaccurate information. It's important for religious educators to ensure their content is well-researched and verified.

input_text: Pegawai kerajaan kerja macam siput. Pergi pejabat pukul 8, tutup kaunter pukul 4, tengah2 tu entah buat apa. Gaji buta
reason: Mocks civil servants as slow ('siput'/snail) and calls their pay 'gaji buta' (pay for doing nothing). While service complaints are valid, the sweeping dismissal disrespects all civil servants.
suggested_rewrite: Perkhidmatan di pejabat kerajaan kadang-kadang lambat dan perlu ditambah baik. Mungkin boleh tingkatkan kecekapan dengan lebih banyak kaunter atau sistem digital.

input_text: Government servants really the best la. Come late, go back early, lunch 2 hours. No wonder nothing gets done in this country
reason: Sarcastic sweeping criticism of all government workers. While efficiency concerns are valid, the mocking generalization disrespects the many dedicated civil servants.
suggested_rewrite: Some government offices could improve their efficiency and service hours. Better systems and accountability would help ensure public services run more smoothly.

input_text: 公务员做工真的很慢，去一个部门办事情要等半天。难怪马来西亚发展这么慢
reason: Blames national development pace entirely on civil servants' slow work. The overgeneralization and causal link between individual service speed and national progress is dismissive.
suggested_rewrite: 政府部门的办事效率有提升的空间。引入更多数字化服务可能有助于加快流程，提升民众的体验。

input_text: Aiya gomen servant, 9am baru sampai office, 11am already coffee break, 12 lunch, 3pm tea time, 4.30 balik. Best job ever la
reason: Exaggerated sarcastic schedule mocking civil servants. While wrapped in humor, it reinforces negative stereotypes about government workers as a whole.
suggested_rewrite: Sometimes government office hours and service speed can be frustrating. It would help if there were clearer service level commitments.

input_text: Dengar cerita menteri tu beli rumah baru lagi. Tak tau la betul ke tak tapi kalau betul memang gila la. Gaji menteri mana cukup beli rumah macam tu
reason: Spreads an unverified rumor about a minister's property purchase with the qualifier 'tak tau betul ke tak'. Even with the disclaimer, repeating unverified claims about public figures can damage reputations.
suggested_rewrite: Ada dakwaan mengenai pembelian hartanah oleh menteri tersebut. Perkara ini patut disiasat oleh pihak berkuasa yang berkenaan untuk memastikan ketelusan.

input_text: Not sure true or not but someone said the MB is using government money for personal stuff. If true then really teruk la
reason: Spreads a rumor about misuse of public funds with weak disclaimer. The 'not sure true or not' framing doesn't prevent potential damage from the unverified allegation.
suggested_rewrite: There are unverified claims about the MB's use of government funds. Such allegations should be investigated through proper channels rather than spread as rumors.

input_text: Korang dengar tak pasal scandal tu? Katanya involve orang besar. Aku forward je, tak tau betul ke tak
reason: Forwards an unverified scandal claim with minimal disclaimer. The 'aku forward je' attitude normalizes spreading unverified information that could harm individuals.
suggested_rewrite: Ada khabar angin tentang skandal yang melibatkan orang berpengaruh. Lebih baik tunggu pengesahan rasmi sebelum menyebarkan maklumat yang belum disahkan.

input_text: 听说那个部长贪污了几百万，不知道是不是真的。但是无风不起浪嘛
reason: Spreads corruption allegation with the 'no smoke without fire' (无风不起浪) framing which lends undue credibility to an unverified claim.
suggested_rewrite: 有关于某部长贪污的传闻。这类指控应该通过正式渠道调查核实，而不是凭传言下定论。

input_text: Weh bro dengar tak, katanya politician X tu kena tangkap semalam. Entah betul ke fake news. Tapi kalau betul memang padan muka la
reason: Spreads unverified arrest claim about a named politician with schadenfreude ('padan muka'). The rumor-spreading combined with gleeful tone is problematic even with the disclaimer.
suggested_rewrite: Ada khabar tentang tangkapan seorang ahli politik. Kita perlu tunggu pengesahan rasmi dan biarkan proses undang-undang berjalan sebelum membuat sebarang kesimpulan.

input_text: Makcik sebelah rumah tu masak apa entah, bau sampai naik loya. Nak cakap takut kena label racist pulak. Tapi seriously la, boleh tak masak kat dalam sikit
reason: Complains about neighbor's cooking smell while acknowledging the racial sensitivity, yet the disgust framing ('naik loya') toward another culture's food is dismissive and culturally insensitive.
suggested_rewrite: Bau masakan jiran kadang-kadang agak kuat. Mungkin saya boleh berbincang secara baik tentang pengudaraan atau cara mengurangkan bau yang meresap.

input_text: Hari Raya ok la tapi kenapa kena invite satu kampung? Parking penuh, jalan sesak. Every year same problem. Tak boleh control sikit ke
reason: Complains about Hari Raya open house culture with dismissive tone. While traffic/parking is a valid concern, questioning the tradition of inviting many guests is culturally insensitive.
suggested_rewrite: Hari Raya memang meriah, cuma mungkin perlu ada perancangan parking yang lebih baik untuk mengurangkan kesesakan jalan di kawasan perumahan.

input_text: CNY firecrackers every year same thing. Like hello, it's illegal also still want to do. Some people really think rules don't apply to them
reason: Frames CNY firecracker tradition purely as law-breaking without acknowledging cultural significance. The 'some people' framing and dismissive tone toward a cultural practice is insensitive.
suggested_rewrite: I understand firecrackers are part of CNY tradition, but safety concerns are valid. Perhaps communities could organize designated areas for celebrations to balance tradition and safety.

input_text: Deepavali dah la oil lamp everywhere fire hazard, then the kolam thing block the corridor also. Aiya common area la, think of others can or not
reason: Dismisses Deepavali decorations (oil lamps, kolam) as hazards and nuisances. While shared space concerns exist, the frustrated tone disregards the festival's cultural importance.
suggested_rewrite: Deepavali decorations are beautiful! For safety in shared spaces, maybe we can coordinate where oil lamps and kolam are placed so everyone can enjoy the festivities safely.

input_text: Haa meme ni memang tepat. Gambar menteri tidur kat parlimen. Caption: 'Gaji RM50k sebulan tapi kerja tidur'. Lawak tapi sedih sebenarnya
reason: Shares/describes a mocking meme of a minister sleeping in parliament with sarcastic salary comparison. While parliamentary accountability matters, the meme format reduces discourse to mockery.
suggested_rewrite: Gambar ahli parlimen yang tidak memberi perhatian semasa sidang memang mengecewakan. Wakil rakyat sepatutnya lebih komited terhadap tanggungjawab mereka di Dewan.

input_text: This minister's face when asked about the scandal LMAOOO 🤣🤣🤣 bro couldn't even string a sentence together. And this is our leader? We're doomed
reason: Mocks a minister's verbal stumble with excessive laughter and doom-saying. While politicians should be articulate, the ridicule and 'we're doomed' hyperbole cross into disrespectful territory.
suggested_rewrite: The minister's response to questions about the scandal was unconvincing and inarticulate. Our leaders need to communicate more clearly and address public concerns directly.

input_text: Tengok la video YB tu cakap BM pun tunggang langgang. Dah la wakil rakyat, cakap pun tak betul. Malu je
reason: Mocks a representative's Malay language ability. While language competence matters for public servants, the tone is ridiculing rather than constructive, and could have ethnic undertones.
suggested_rewrite: Wakil rakyat sepatutnya berkomunikasi dengan baik dalam Bahasa Malaysia. Mungkin latihan bahasa boleh membantu meningkatkan keupayaan mereka berkhidmat.

input_text: Haiya this politician ah, every time kena tanya soalan susah sure pandai dodge. Slippery like eel la this fella
reason: Compares a politician to an eel (slippery) for dodging questions. The animal comparison and dismissive tone are disrespectful though the accountability concern is valid.
suggested_rewrite: This politician often avoids answering difficult questions directly. Citizens deserve transparent and straightforward responses from their elected representatives.

input_text: Colleague aku satu ni ha, datang kerja lambat balik awal. Boss tak tegur sebab dia pandai bodek. Memang la culture dorang macam tu
reason: Complains about a colleague's work ethic but ends with 'culture dorang macam tu' which attributes the individual's behavior to their group's culture, a form of stereotyping.
suggested_rewrite: Colleague saya ni kurang disiplin dalam soal waktu kerja. Saya rasa pihak pengurusan perlu lebih tegas dalam menguatkuasakan peraturan kerja untuk semua.

input_text: Office mate always take MC on Monday and Friday. We all know la she just malas. But because she pandai act cute with boss nothing happens. Typical la
reason: Accuses a colleague of faking sick leave and manipulating the boss. The gossipy tone and 'typical la' dismissal are disrespectful and could create a hostile workplace environment.
suggested_rewrite: A colleague frequently takes leave on Mondays and Fridays, which affects the team's workload. Management should review attendance patterns and ensure fair enforcement of leave policies.

input_text: Eh kalau masuk je opis kerajaan confirm nampak semua tengah scroll phone. Rakyat bayar cukai untuk dorang main TikTok ke? Hahaha
reason: Sarcastically claims all government office workers are on their phones watching TikTok. The sweeping mockery disrespects civil servants while raising a valid productivity concern.
suggested_rewrite: Penggunaan telefon bimbit semasa waktu kerja di pejabat kerajaan patut dikurangkan untuk memastikan perkhidmatan yang lebih cekap kepada rakyat.

input_text: Dah 2026 tapi government department still guna sistem zaman batu. Then complain rakyat tak datang buat urusan. Pelik betul la korang ni
reason: Mocks government IT systems as 'stone age' and addresses civil servants with dismissive 'korang ni'. The frustration with outdated systems is valid but the tone is unnecessarily antagonistic.
suggested_rewrite: Sistem digital di jabatan kerajaan perlu dikemas kini untuk memudahkan urusan rakyat. Pelaburan dalam teknologi moden boleh meningkatkan kecekapan perkhidmatan awam.

input_text: Weh aku nak rant sikit. Parking kat apartment dah la sikit, jiran2 satu family 4 kereta semua nak park. Selfish gila. Dah la bising, skrg parking pun rampas
reason: Angry rant about parking but 'jiran satu family' and combining noise + parking complaints creates a profile that could target a specific family/community. 'Selfish gila' is aggressive.
suggested_rewrite: Masalah parking di apartment saya makin teruk bila ada keluarga yang ada banyak kereta. Mungkin pihak pengurusan perlu tetapkan had parking per unit.

input_text: Nak renovate rumah pun kena ikut permit la apa la. Tapi jiran boleh pulak buat extension sesuka hati takde siapa tegur. Double standard betul
reason: Implies unequal enforcement of rules with frustration. While fair enforcement is a valid concern, the 'double standard' framing without evidence could fuel communal grievance narratives.
suggested_rewrite: Peraturan renovasi perlu dikuatkuasakan secara adil untuk semua penduduk. Jika ada pelanggaran, ia patut dilaporkan kepada pihak berkuasa tempatan.

input_text: What's the point of voting la, all same same. BN ke PH ke PN ke, masuk je confirm lupa rakyat. Baik aku tak keluar rumah next election
reason: Expresses deep political cynicism and threatens voter abstention. While frustration is valid, the nihilistic 'all the same' framing and encouraging non-participation undermines democratic process.
suggested_rewrite: Saya faham perasaan kecewa dengan politik, tapi undi kita tetap penting. Kita perlu terus menuntut perubahan melalui proses demokrasi dan memilih calon yang benar-benar komited.

input_text: Entah la aku dah give up dengan Malaysia ni. Semua pun nak kena kabel, nak kena kenal orang. Kalau takde cable memang mampus la
reason: Expresses despair about cronyism using crude language ('mampus'). The frustration about connections-based culture is widely shared but the nihilistic framing is counterproductive.
suggested_rewrite: Budaya 'cable' dan kronisme di Malaysia memang mengecewakan. Kita perlu sistem yang lebih telus dan berasaskan merit untuk memberi peluang yang adil kepada semua.

input_text: Ustaz tu cakap pasal dosa orang lain je. Diri sendiri tak check ke? Perangai macam malaikat tapi entah la belakang tabir macam mana
reason: Insinuates hypocrisy of a religious teacher with veiled accusation ('belakang tabir'). While questioning hypocrisy is valid, the speculative tone about a religious figure's private life touches on religious and personal sensitivity.
suggested_rewrite: Pemuka agama sepatutnya memberi contoh yang baik dan tidak hanya menghukum orang lain. Penting untuk mereka mengamalkan apa yang diajarkan.

input_text: 那个宗教讲师在网上讲了很多极端的东西。这种人不应该有平台。脑子有问题的
reason: Calls a religious speaker mentally deficient ('脑子有问题') and demands deplatforming. While concern about extremist rhetoric is valid, the personal insult and dismissive approach is problematic.
suggested_rewrite: 这位宗教讲师的一些言论令人担忧。相关平台和宗教当局应该审查其内容是否符合正确的宗教教导。

input_text: Ceramah kat surau tu asyik cakap pasal politik je. Dah la tempat ibadat, boleh tak jangan campur aduk. Menyampah aku
reason: Expresses disgust ('menyampah') about political content in religious talks. While separation of religion and politics is debatable, the disgust framing about a religious setting is insensitive.
suggested_rewrite: Saya rasa ceramah di surau sepatutnya lebih fokus kepada ilmu agama dan bukan politik. Tempat ibadat perlu kekal sebagai ruang spiritual untuk semua.

input_text: Semua orang pun tau la siapa yang control ekonomi kat Malaysia ni. Kita ni nak dapat apa pun susah
reason: Uses coded language ('siapa yang control ekonomi') which is a well-known dog whistle targeting a specific community. The vague phrasing allows plausible deniability while reinforcing economic stereotypes.
suggested_rewrite: Peluang ekonomi di Malaysia perlu diagihkan dengan lebih adil. Semua rakyat tanpa mengira latar belakang patut mendapat peluang yang sama untuk berjaya.

input_text: Biasa la negara kita, everything pun about who you know not what you know. Certain people always get the contract one, others just watch la
reason: 'Certain people always get the contract' uses coded language suggesting a specific group benefits unfairly. While cronyism concerns are valid, the framing edges toward communal resentment.
suggested_rewrite: Government contracts should be awarded transparently based on merit and capability, not connections. A fairer procurement system would benefit everyone.

input_text: Pasar malam dah la kotor, harga pun mahal gila. Yang jual tu semua nak untung je, tak kisah kualiti langsung. Orang luar pulak yang ambil kesempatan
reason: 'Orang luar yang ambil kesempatan' (outsiders taking advantage) is a coded reference that could target foreign workers or specific communities, adding xenophobic undertones to a market complaint.
suggested_rewrite: Harga dan kebersihan pasar malam perlu dipantau oleh pihak berkuasa tempatan. Semua peniaga perlu mematuhi standard kebersihan dan harga yang berpatutan.

input_text: Asyik2 kena bayar cukai tapi service macam sampah. Hospital kerajaan tunggu 6 jam, road penuh lubang. Duit pergi mana??
reason: Compares government services to 'sampah' (trash) while demanding accountability for tax money. The frustration is valid but the aggressive tone and trash comparison are disrespectful.
suggested_rewrite: Rakyat membayar cukai dan berhak mendapat perkhidmatan yang baik. Masa menunggu di hospital kerajaan dan penyelenggaraan jalan raya perlu ditambah baik segera.

input_text: 真的受不了，排了三个小时的队结果柜台关了。政府部门做事就是这样，完全不在乎人民的时间
reason: Sweeping complaint that government departments don't care about people's time. While the service experience is frustrating, the generalization dismisses all civil servants.
suggested_rewrite: 排了三个小时结果柜台关了，这种体验确实令人沮丧。政府部门应该改善排队系统和服务时间管理。

input_text: இந்த நாட்டில் சாதாரண மக்களுக்கு நீதி கிடைக்காது. பணக்காரர்களுக்கு ஒரு சட்டம், ஏழைகளுக்கு ஒரு சட்டம்
reason: Claims there is no justice for ordinary people and alleges a two-tier legal system. While inequality concerns are valid, the absolute framing ('no justice at all') is emotionally loaded and undermines trust in institutions.
suggested_rewrite: நீதி அமைப்பு அனைவருக்கும் சமமாக இருக்க வேண்டும். செல்வந்தர்களுக்கும் ஏழைகளுக்கும் ஒரே சட்டம் பயன்படுத்தப்பட வேண்டும் என்பதை உறுதி செய்ய வேண்டும்.

input_text: Kalau kerja gomen confirm la senang. Pukul 5 sharp dah boleh balik. Private sector kita kerja macam kuda baru survive
reason: Sarcastically portrays government work as easy with a comparison to 'working like a horse' in private sector. The comparison unfairly dismisses the work of civil servants.
suggested_rewrite: Ada perbezaan budaya kerja antara sektor kerajaan dan swasta. Kedua-dua sektor ada cabaran tersendiri dan sumbangan masing-masing perlu dihargai.

input_text: Dulu cikgu kena hormat, sekarang ustaz pun jadi influencer TikTok. Content mengarut tapi follower berjuta. Memalukan profession
reason: Dismisses religious teachers on social media as producing 'mengarut' (nonsensical) content and shaming the profession. While quality concerns are valid, the blanket dismissal is disrespectful.
suggested_rewrite: Pemuka agama yang menggunakan media sosial perlu memastikan kandungan mereka berkualiti dan bermanfaat. Platform digital boleh jadi alat dakwah yang baik jika digunakan dengan bertanggungjawab.

input_text: Bro aku forward ni, group lain share. Katanya ada kes rasuah besar tapi media semua tutup. Entah betul ke tak tapi suspicious gila
reason: Forwards an unverified corruption claim with media cover-up allegation. The chain-forwarding behavior and conspiracy framing ('media semua tutup') spreads potential misinformation.
suggested_rewrite: Ada dakwaan tentang kes rasuah yang belum disahkan. Jika ada bukti, ia patut dilaporkan kepada SPRM atau media yang bertanggungjawab, bukan disebarkan tanpa pengesahan.

input_text: Eh korang share2 la story ni. Budak viral tu kena buli sebab post pasal scandal. Sian dia, expose je la semua. Dorang ingat boleh cover up ke
reason: Encourages viral sharing of unverified scandal content and dismisses privacy concerns. The mob-justice mentality ('expose je la semua') can lead to harm even if intentions seem righteous.
suggested_rewrite: Jika ada kes yang perlu didedahkan, ia patut dilaporkan kepada pihak berkuasa yang berkenaan. Menyebarkan tanpa pengesahan boleh membahayakan orang yang terlibat.

input_text: You all see that minister's answer in parliament or not? Like primary school kid answering teacher's question. So embarrassing for the country
reason: Compares a minister's parliamentary answer to a primary school child, which is demeaning. The core concern about quality of parliamentary discourse is valid but expressed mockingly.
suggested_rewrite: The minister's response in parliament was underwhelming and lacked substance. Parliamentarians should prepare thoroughly to address questions with the seriousness they deserve.

input_text: Tiap kali hujan mesti banjir. Longkang pun tak boleh maintain. Majlis perbandaran buat apa entah. Makan gaji buta je semua
reason: Calls municipal workers 'gaji buta' (paid for nothing) over drainage issues. While infrastructure complaints are valid, the sweeping insult dismisses all council workers.
suggested_rewrite: Masalah banjir kilat berulang menunjukkan penyelenggaraan longkang perlu ditambah baik. Majlis perbandaran perlu memprioritikan infrastruktur saliran.

input_text: Makin lama makin banyak kedai tutup, economy memang teruk. Tapi orang atas still hidup mewah. Rakyat marhain macam kita ni suffer je la
reason: Frames economic hardship with an us-vs-them (rakyat vs elites) narrative. While inequality is a valid concern, the resentful framing can fuel class-based antagonism.
suggested_rewrite: Penutupan banyak kedai menunjukkan ekonomi perlu perhatian segera. Kerajaan perlu memastikan dasar ekonomi yang lebih adil dan membantu golongan yang terkesan.

input_text: Confirm la projek tu dapat kat kroni dia. Kita ni rakyat biasa mana ada chance. Sistem dah rosak, tak payah harap
reason: Makes unverified cronyism claim and expresses hopelessness about the system. While concerns about fair procurement exist, the absolute framing ('sistem dah rosak') undermines trust without evidence.
suggested_rewrite: Proses tender projek kerajaan perlu lebih telus dan kompetitif. Rakyat berhak menuntut akauntabiliti dalam perbelanjaan awam.

input_text: Local council people come and check restaurant also no use. Give kopi money then everything settle. That's Malaysia for you
reason: Alleges bribery in local council enforcement without evidence. While corruption concerns exist, casually normalizing bribery as 'that's Malaysia' spreads a cynical unverified narrative.
suggested_rewrite: If there are issues with enforcement integrity at the local council level, they should be reported to the relevant anti-corruption authorities. We should not normalize unethical practices.

input_text: 每次大选都是一样的故事。说来说去都是种族牌。这些政客真的把我们当傻子
reason: Accuses politicians of playing the race card and treating voters as fools. While concern about racial politics is valid, the 'treating us as idiots' framing is emotionally loaded and disrespectful.
suggested_rewrite: 政治人物不应该利用种族议题来争取选票。选民希望看到以政策为基础的竞选，而非分裂社会的策略。

input_text: Ambulance pun stuck in traffic and government still want to build more condo. Priorities totally wrong la. Who is running this circus??
reason: Calls governance a 'circus' while raising valid concerns about traffic and development priorities. The dismissive metaphor undermines constructive engagement with urban planning issues.
suggested_rewrite: When ambulances get stuck in traffic, it shows that urban planning needs serious attention. The government should prioritize infrastructure and emergency access over new developments.

input_text: That influencer ustazah is so cringe la. Selling skincare one minute, giving religious advice the next. Pick a lane la sis
reason: Mocks a religious figure's dual role as influencer and religious teacher with dismissive 'cringe' and 'pick a lane'. While mixing commerce and religion raises valid questions, the mocking tone is disrespectful.
suggested_rewrite: When religious figures mix commercial promotions with religious content, it can raise questions about credibility. Religious educators should be mindful of maintaining their authority and trust.

input_text: இந்த ஊரில் தமிழ் பள்ளிக்கு நிதி கேட்டால் யாரும் கவனிக்க மாட்டார்கள். வேறு பள்ளிக்கு உடனே கொடுப்பார்கள். இது என்ன நியாயம்?
reason: Alleges unequal school funding favoring certain schools over Tamil schools. While education equity is a valid concern, the framing implies deliberate discrimination without providing specific evidence.
suggested_rewrite: தமிழ்ப் பள்ளிகளுக்கான நிதி ஒதுக்கீடு குறித்த கவலைகள் உள்ளன. அனைத்து வகை பள்ளிகளுக்கும் நியாயமான நிதி வழங்கப்பட வேண்டும்.

input_text: 为什么华小要自己筹款，其他学校政府全部包？这个国家对我们公平吗？
reason: Claims Chinese schools are unfairly required to fundraise while others are fully funded. The rhetorical question about fairness frames the issue as deliberate discrimination, feeding communal grievance.
suggested_rewrite: 华小的拨款问题值得关注。政府应确保所有类型的学校都获得公平合理的资金支持。

input_text: Eh dah 2026, masih lagi gaduh pasal bahasa. Nak English ke BM ke, make up your mind la. Pening kepala rakyat je
reason: Dismisses the national language policy debate with frustration. While the concern is practical, the dismissive tone oversimplifies an important cultural and educational issue.
suggested_rewrite: Polisi bahasa dalam pendidikan perlu keputusan yang jelas dan konsisten. Rakyat memerlukan kepastian supaya anak-anak kita dapat manfaat terbaik dari sistem pendidikan.

input_text: Group chat uncle strikes again with the conspiracy theory. Now say water supply kena poison. These boomers really believe everything they read on WhatsApp
reason: Dismisses older people as gullible 'boomers' while referencing an unverified conspiracy about water supply poisoning. The ageist framing and mention of the conspiracy both contribute to problems.
suggested_rewrite: Unverified claims about water supply contamination are circulating online. It's important for everyone to verify information from official sources before sharing, regardless of age.

input_text: Haiya traffic jam from 7am to 10am, then 4pm to 8pm. We basically live in our cars la. Government collect road tax for what? For us to park on highway ah?
reason: Sarcastic complaint about traffic and road tax with rhetorical questions. While traffic frustration is valid, the sarcastic tone toward government spending is dismissive rather than constructive.
suggested_rewrite: The daily traffic congestion from morning to evening significantly impacts quality of life. The government needs to invest road tax revenue into better public transport and traffic solutions.

input_text: Makin hari makin ramai pekerja asing, local pun tak dapat kerja. Then wonder why crime rate naik. Connect the dots la
reason: Implies a causal link between foreign workers, local unemployment, and crime without evidence. The 'connect the dots' framing encourages xenophobic conclusions while maintaining plausible deniability.
suggested_rewrite: The management of foreign workers and local employment opportunities needs better policy coordination. Crime rates should be addressed through proper law enforcement rather than blaming any group.

input_text: 每次去政府部门办事都要等很久，工作人员还态度很差。问多一点就给你黑脸。服务精神在哪里？
reason: Complains about long waits and poor attitude at government offices. While service quality concerns are valid, generalizing all workers as having bad attitudes is a sweeping negative judgment.
suggested_rewrite: 政府部门的服务态度和效率有待改善。推行服务评分系统可能有助于提升公务员的服务品质。

input_text: இந்த அரசாங்க ஊழியர்கள் மக்களுக்கு சேவை செய்ய வேண்டும் என்று மறந்துவிட்டார்கள். அவர்கள் நினைப்பது எல்லாம் சம்பளம் மட்டுமே
reason: Claims government workers have forgotten their duty to serve and only think about salary. The sweeping accusation disrespects all civil servants based on some negative experiences.
suggested_rewrite: அரசாங்க ஊழியர்கள் மக்களுக்கு சிறந்த சேவையை வழங்க வேண்டும். சேவை தரத்தை மேம்படுத்த பயிற்சி மற்றும் கண்காணிப்பு முக்கியம்.

input_text: Why la Malaysian drivers so terrible one. Especially those big car drivers, think they own the road. Signal also lazy to use. Bodoh
reason: Generalizes all Malaysian drivers as terrible and uses 'bodoh' (stupid) as a closing insult. While road safety concerns are valid, the sweeping insults and class-adjacent jab at 'big car drivers' are problematic.
suggested_rewrite: Road safety in Malaysia needs improvement, especially signal usage. All drivers, regardless of vehicle type, should practice courtesy and follow traffic rules.

input_text: JKR roads in my area full of potholes. My car rosak already because of this. When I complain they say 'dalam perancangan'. Perancangan apa, 10 tahun pun tak siap
reason: Sarcastically mocks JKR's 'dalam perancangan' (under planning) response about potholes. While infrastructure frustration is valid, the mocking of official communication undermines constructive engagement.
suggested_rewrite: The potholes on JKR roads in my area have caused damage to my vehicle. The repair timeline needs to be more transparent and accountable to residents.

input_text: 看看现在的年轻人，整天刷手机，不想工作。然后怪政府没有给机会。自己不努力还怪谁？
reason: Sweeping generalization about young people as lazy phone addicts who blame the government. While work ethic concerns exist, the blanket dismissal of youth challenges is disrespectful and ageist.
suggested_rewrite: 年轻人在当前经济环境中面临不少挑战。虽然个人努力很重要，但政府也需提供更多就业培训和机会来支持青年发展。

input_text: இளைஞர்கள் எல்லாம் சோம்பேறிகள். வேலை செய்ய விரும்பவில்லை, ஆனால் ஐபோன் வேண்டும். யாரிடம் இருந்து கற்றார்கள் இதை?
reason: Labels all youth as lazy (சோம்பேறிகள்) and materialistic. The rhetorical question implies blame on the generation. Sweeping negative generalization about an entire demographic.
suggested_rewrite: இன்றைய இளைஞர்கள் பல சவால்களை எதிர்கொள்கிறார்கள். வேலைவாய்ப்பு மற்றும் நிதி கல்வியறிவை மேம்படுத்துவது அனைவருக்கும் பயனளிக்கும்.

input_text: Kids nowadays cannot take criticism at all. Say one thing they cry and report to HR. Snowflake generation betul la. How to compete globally like this
reason: Dismisses younger workers as 'snowflakes' who can't handle criticism. While generational workplace dynamics are real, the derisive labeling and catastrophizing about global competitiveness is disrespectful.
suggested_rewrite: Different generations may have different approaches to workplace feedback. Building a culture of constructive criticism benefits both experienced and newer workers.

input_text: My area got so many stray dogs already, council do nothing. Someone kena bite then only want to act. Typical la Malaysian government, wait until got problem baru nak move
reason: Criticizes council's reactive approach to stray dogs with 'typical la Malaysian government' generalization. While the safety concern is valid, the sweeping dismissal of governance is unnecessarily cynical.
suggested_rewrite: The stray dog population in my area is a growing safety concern. The local council should implement a proactive animal control program before any incidents occur.

input_text: Raja2 kita ni hidup mewah, rakyat struggle nak bayar bil. At least show some solidarity la. Tak pernah pun nampak turun padang untuk real
reason: Criticizes royalty's lifestyle and perceived lack of community engagement. While criticism of public spending is valid, the tone toward the constitutional monarchy lacks the expected courtesy (adab).
suggested_rewrite: Institusi raja-raja boleh memainkan peranan lebih aktif dalam membantu rakyat yang memerlukan. Penglibatan langsung dalam isu kebajikan rakyat akan mengukuhkan kepercayaan terhadap institusi.

input_text: Sultan beli kereta baru lagi. Meanwhile hospital kerajaan tak cukup katil. Where is the justice in this country?
reason: Contrasts royal spending with healthcare deficiencies in an accusatory way. The comparison creates an unfair juxtaposition since budgets come from different sources, and the tone toward the ruler is disrespectful.
suggested_rewrite: Healthcare funding remains a critical concern, with hospitals needing more resources. All public spending, including institutional budgets, should prioritize the welfare of the people.

input_text: 那些有钱有势的人犯了法也没事，普通人就倒霉了。这个国家的法律到底保护谁？
reason: Claims the law only protects the wealthy and powerful. While justice equity is a valid concern, the absolute framing that ordinary people always suffer undermines institutional trust.
suggested_rewrite: 法律面前人人平等是法治社会的基本原则。司法系统应确保每个人无论贫富都获得公正对待。

input_text: Temple next to my house every day burn incense until my whole house smelly. I respect their religion but can they consider others also or not?
reason: Describes incense from a temple as making the house 'smelly'. While the inconvenience is real, describing religious practices as smelly is culturally insensitive despite the respect caveat.
suggested_rewrite: The incense from the nearby temple sometimes affects air quality at my home. Perhaps we could have a friendly conversation with the temple management about timing or ventilation solutions.

input_text: கோவிலில் ஒலிபெருக்கி மிகவும் சத்தமாக இருக்கிறது. நான் மதத்தை மதிக்கிறேன், ஆனால் இது மற்றவர்களை தொந்தரவு செய்கிறது. கொஞ்சம் குறைக்க முடியாதா?
reason: Complains about temple loudspeaker volume. While the concern about noise is legitimate, framing religious music/prayers as 'disturbing others' requires sensitive handling, and the tone is somewhat curt.
suggested_rewrite: கோவில் ஒலிபெருக்கி சற்று சத்தமாக இருக்கிறது. கோவில் நிர்வாகத்துடன் இணக்கமான முறையில் இதை பேசி ஒரு தீர்வு காணலாம்.

input_text: Malaysian public toilet is a disgrace. Always dirty, no soap, tissue also no. Even in shopping mall also like that. Third world mentality
reason: Labels dirty public toilets as 'third world mentality', a derogatory and loaded term. While cleanliness standards need improvement, the framing is condescending and disrespectful to the nation.
suggested_rewrite: Public toilet cleanliness in Malaysia needs significant improvement, even in shopping malls. Consistent maintenance schedules and accountability measures would make a big difference.

input_text: Wow so now even hawker food RM15 already. Thank you very much to whoever is running this economy. Hidup rakyat makin susah but sure their salary naik kan
reason: Sarcastic 'thank you' about rising food prices with implied accusation about leaders' salaries. The cost of living concern is very real but the sarcastic and accusatory tone is problematic.
suggested_rewrite: Harga makanan di gerai telah meningkat dengan ketara. Kerajaan perlu mengkaji semula langkah-langkah kawalan harga dan membantu meringankan beban kos sara hidup rakyat.

input_text: 这些外卖送餐员骑摩托车像疯子一样。迟早会出事的。他们不珍惜自己的命吗？
reason: Calls delivery riders 'crazy' (疯子) and questions if they value their lives. While road safety is a valid concern, the derogatory language and rhetorical question are dismissive of gig workers' pressured conditions.
suggested_rewrite: 外卖骑手的驾驶安全问题令人担忧。平台公司和政府应共同制定更好的安全规范，保护骑手和其他道路使用者。

input_text: இந்த நகரத்தில் தமிழ் படிக்க இடமே இல்லை. எல்லா இடத்திலும் வேறு மொழிக்கு முன்னுரிமை. நம் மொழி அழிந்து போகும் போல
reason: Claims Tamil language is being neglected in favor of other languages with dramatic framing that the language will 'die out'. While language preservation is valid, the victimhood narrative and accusatory tone can fuel communal tensions.
suggested_rewrite: தமிழ் மொழிக் கல்விக்கான வாய்ப்புகள் இந்த நகரத்தில் அதிகரிக்கப்பட வேண்டும். அனைத்து தாய்மொழிகளும் பாதுகாக்கப்பட வேண்டும் என்பது அரசின் கடமை.

input_text: 大选前说什么都好听，选完了就消失。马来西亚的政治真的让人心寒。这些政客把我们当什么？
reason: Rhetorically asks what politicians take voters for, implying contempt. The emotional language ('心寒'/chilling) and confrontational rhetorical question express valid frustration in an aggrieved tone.
suggested_rewrite: 大选后政治人物应该积极兑现承诺。选民有权要求当选者对他们的竞选承诺负责。

input_text: தேர்தல் முடிந்துவிட்டது, இப்போது யாரும் எங்களைப் பற்றி கவலைப்படுவதில்லை. வாக்குகளை திருடிவிட்டார்கள், அவ்வளவுதான்
reason: Claims votes were 'stolen' (திருடிவிட்டார்கள்) without evidence, undermining election integrity. While post-election disillusionment is understandable, unsubstantiated stolen-vote claims are dangerous.
suggested_rewrite: தேர்தலுக்குப் பிறகு மக்களின் குரல் கேட்கப்படவில்லை என்ற உணர்வு இருக்கிறது. தேர்தல் செயல்முறையின் நேர்மை குறித்த கவலைகள் இருந்தால், முறையான புகார் வழிகளை பயன்படுத்த வேண்டும்.

input_text: Bruh imagine paying road tax every year and still got potholes everywhere. The money go where? Into someone's pocket la obviously. Malaysia memang boleh 🙄
reason: Implies road tax money is being pocketed ('into someone's pocket obviously') without evidence. While infrastructure concerns are valid, the casual corruption allegation with sarcastic 'Malaysia boleh' is problematic.
suggested_rewrite: Road tax collection should translate to well-maintained roads. The public deserves transparency on how road maintenance funds are allocated and spent.

input_text: இந்த ஊரில் பேருந்து சேவை மிகவும் மோசம். ஒரு மணி நேரம் காத்திருந்தும் வரவில்லை. இந்த அரசாங்கத்திற்கு மக்கள் பற்றி கவலையே இல்லை
reason: Claims the government doesn't care about people based on poor bus service. While public transport needs improvement, the absolute claim that government has no concern for people is an emotionally loaded overgeneralization.
suggested_rewrite: பேருந்து சேவையின் தரம் மேம்படுத்தப்பட வேண்டும். வழக்கமான மற்றும் நம்பகமான போக்குவரத்து சேவை மக்களின் அடிப்படை தேவை.

input_text: Haish this grabfood rider lambat gila bro. Order pukul 12, sampai pukul 1.30. Makanan dah sejuk, then still expect 5 star rating. Dream on la
reason: Mocks a delivery rider with dismissive tone. While late delivery is frustrating, the contemptuous framing ignores the difficult conditions gig workers face.
suggested_rewrite: Penghantaran makanan saya lewat lebih sejam dan makanan sudah sejuk. Platform perlu memperbaiki anggaran masa penghantaran supaya pengalaman pelanggan dan pemandu lebih baik.

input_text: Dah la toll naik, petrol naik, everything naik. Only thing yang tak naik is our salary. Government really don't care about middle class la. We just ATM machine for them
reason: Calls the middle class an 'ATM machine' for the government, implying exploitation. While cost of living frustrations are real, the dehumanizing metaphor and absolute 'don't care' framing is counterproductive.
suggested_rewrite: Kenaikan kos sara hidup tanpa kenaikan gaji yang setimpal membebankan golongan pertengahan. Kerajaan perlu mendengar masalah rakyat dan memperkenalkan langkah pelepasan yang berkesan.

input_text: Wah bestnya jadi politician kan. Cuti panjang, gaji tinggi, free travel. Rakyat biasa kerja sampai pengsan pun tak cukup makan. This country is run by jokers la seriously
reason: Sarcastically envies politicians' perceived benefits while calling them 'jokers'. The sarcastic framing and sweeping dismissal reduce legitimate governance criticism to mockery.
suggested_rewrite: Perbandingan antara faedah ahli politik dan realiti kehidupan rakyat biasa menimbulkan persoalan tentang keadilan. Pemimpin perlu lebih peka dan bertanggungjawab terhadap kesejahteraan rakyat.
